title: Action manifest — minimal request to run something
description: JSON body submitted to POST /api/actions. The server validates the manifest, dispatches to the named runner (a per-atlas Python module), then runs the matching extractor to wrap the raw output in a layer envelope. Every accepted manifest is appended to registry/actions.log.jsonl. Kept intentionally small per PIPELINE_FLOW.md §'Minimal stable core'. Atlases may extend with extra top-level fields — additionalProperties is true.

type.description: Action kind (e.g. 'run_popstats', 'import_raw_result', 'normalize_layer'). Maps 1:1 to a runner in the atlas's dispatcher.

dataset_id.description: Cohort_id (HIERARCHY_SPEC) or species_id (legacy). Scopes the action and its produced layers.

runner.description: Runner identifier; either a registry name in <atlas>/registries/data/actions.registry.json or a python module path like 'runners.popstats.run_fst'.

target.description: What slice the action runs against (chrom, start_bp, end_bp, groups, candidate_id, etc.). Runner-specific; validated against schema_in/<type>_v<N>.schema.json by the dispatcher.

expected_outputs.description: Declared output layer types. The dispatcher uses these to pick the right extractor and to validate the produced envelopes.
